content.problemStatement:
Given an array A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã‚Ä¶AN‚Äã, find the minimum number of operations (possibly zero) required to convert all integers in AAA to 000.

In one operation, you


choose a non-negative integer ppp (p‚â•0p \geq 0p‚â•0),
select at most KKK indices in the array AAA, and
for each selected index iii, replace AiA_iAi‚Äã with Ai‚äï2pA_i\oplus 2^pAi‚Äã‚äï2p. Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes bitwise XOR.
Subtasks
**Subtask #1 (100 points)**: Original Constraints

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN, KKK - the size of the array and the maximum number of elements you can select in an operation.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã‚Ä¶AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations to make all elements of the array 000.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Here is one way to achieve [0,0,0][0, 0, 0][0,0,0] from [3,6,10][3, 6, 10][3,6,10] in 555 operations:

Choose p=0p = 0p=0 and indices {1}\{1\}{1}. Now AAA becomes [2,6,10][2, 6, 10][2,6,10].
Choose p=1p = 1p=1 and indices {1,2}\{1,2\}{1,2}. Now AAA becomes [0,4,10][0, 4, 10][0,4,10].
Choose p=1p = 1p=1 and indices {3}\{3\}{3}. Now AAA becomes [0,4,8][0, 4, 8][0,4,8].
Choose p=2p = 2p=2 and indices {2}\{2\}{2}. Now AAA becomes [0,0,8][0, 0, 8][0,0,8].
Choose p=3p = 3p=3 and indices {3}\{3\}{3}. Now AAA becomes [0,0,0][0, 0, 0][0,0,0].

It can be shown that at least 555 operations are required.
